name: Marc Bech Sørensen
blurb: Danish Superliga's standout defensive midfielder this season, strongly linked with a Championship move all summer — the kind of profile that doesn't stay available long.

name: Nathan Wood
blurb: Boyhood Boro academy product, Ingleby Barwick born. Southampton's relegation has him available — a proper boyhood-return story, and it fills the centre-back depth need.

name: Josh Mulligan
blurb: Breakout season at Hibs has West Ham, Wolves and Southampton all circling. Boro can't win a bidding war on financial muscle alone — worth trying anyway.

name: Daniel Jebbison
blurb: Sheffield United academy, bounced through a Bournemouth-Preston loan last season. Canada international. Half the Championship's chasing the same loan.

name: Dennis Cirkin
blurb: Free transfer, promotion pedigree — five years and 120 games of first-team Championship football, key man in Sunderland's play-off run. As close to a no-brainer as this window gets.

name: David Ozoh
blurb: Genuinely unclear if this is a real move or just noise from the Hackney negotiation — some reports say target, others say smokescreen. Cheap enough to find out.

name: Ronan Kelly
blurb: League of Ireland top scorer, raw as they come. Could be a steal, could be a League of Ireland top scorer and nothing more.

name: Amara Diallo
blurb: Frightening pace on tape, barely any senior minutes. Boom or bust, and mostly untested.

name: Oliver Skipp
blurb: Real reported interest — Boro in talks over the £25m man, but Leicester's £15m asking price is well above his ~£6m market value. Worth pushing on.

name: Kevin Denkey
blurb: The confirmed new-No.9 priority — Boro and Coventry both chasing the $16.2m MLS record signing, 20 goals in 39 for Cincinnati. Ajax, Fenerbahce and Hoffenheim are circling too. Fee's the size of the ambition.

name: Max Arfsten
blurb: USMNT full international, World Cup squad, long-time Boro target across two windows — talks progressing now Targett's loan wasn't made permanent. Would reunite with international teammate Aidan Morris.

name: Kaique Rocha
blurb: Serie B Brazil, all pace and stepovers — end product is years away, if it ever arrives. Cheap enough that nobody minds finding out.

name: Franco Suárez
blurb: Primera Nacional top scorer at 20, physical for his age, finishing still streaky. Agent's been shopping him round the Championship all summer.

name: Elias Bergström
blurb: Allsvenskan regular already at 20 — reads the ball well, footwork behind the level of his positioning. Needs a year of Championship physicality to know what he actually is.

name: Magnus Dahl
blurb: Eliteserien and Conference League minutes already, box-to-box engine, no European club has taken the plunge yet. Boro would be first.

name: Caleb Osei
blurb: Homegrown at Columbus, athletic and quick to recover, minimal experience against anything resembling Championship physicality. Green card sorted, work permit is the only real hurdle.

name: Viktor Lindqvist
blurb: Allsvenskan's tallest keeper this season, huge shot-stopping upside, distribution still raw under pressure. A punt on a body type more than a finished product.

name: Mateo Rojas
blurb: Uruguayan academy graduate, overlaps for fun, defensively still learning the position. Barely 20 senior minutes to his name — pure projection.

name: Jayden Reyes
blurb: MLS Next Pro top scorer, homegrown deal expired without a first-team breakthrough — available cheap as a result. Genuine athleticism, end product unproven above academy level.

name: Otto Rosengren
blurb: One senior Sweden cap, and Boro are reportedly favourites for his signature — but the more sober Teesside reporting has this one likely falling through specifically on work permit eligibility, whatever the interest.

name: Owen Kilbane
blurb: The Championship's most complete right-back not already at a bigger club — two promotion pushes in three years, several sides believed to be circling. Fills the RB need at a genuine premium.

name: Declan Hoy
blurb: Commanding, quick, comfortable stepping into midfield — exactly the level up this defence is missing. A release-clause rumour keeps doing the rounds; whether it's real is the only question.

name: Yusuf Adeyemi
blurb: Direct, two-footed, the kind of Championship winger who gets a bidding war rather than a bargain. Every scout in the division has seen the tape by now.

name: Cody Drameh
blurb: Leeds academy product, been round the loan circuit for years without settling — genuine top-half quality when focused, and reportedly available on a permanent deal at last.

name: Anders Lindberg
blurb: Allsvenskan's breakout full-back this season, overlaps constantly, defensively still catching up to his engine. Scouted more on tape than in person.

name: Divin Mubama
blurb: Sold by West Ham to Man City's academy set-up in 2024, out on loan at Stoke since — genuine reported interest from Boro as that loan winds down. A striker by trade, tried out wide here, raw end product but real underlying numbers.

name: Théo Rousseau
blurb: Ligue 2's most-watched winger this season, agent already shopping him round the Championship — genuinely frightening one-on-one, end product against real physicality still a question mark.

name: Lennon Kirkwood
blurb: Scottish Premiership Player of the Season nominee, direct and left-footed — the kind of step-up move that gets made every summer, several Championship clubs believed to be watching.

name: Baltasar Gomes
blurb: Primeira Liga's most-scouted winger this season — release clause reportedly within reach, several Championship promotion-chasers already circling. Would be a statement signing if it happens.

name: Anton Berg
blurb: Allsvenskan minutes at 19, quick and direct, decision-making still raw. A pure development punt at the price.
